usp: Country Pulse turns public humanitarian data into simple, evidence-led comparisons: what changed, where, compared with what, and why that matters.

questions.label: What is available enough to compare?

countries.name: Sudan

reporting.confidenceLabel: No comparable public value available for this filter.

funding.fundingStatusLabel: No comparable public value available for this filter.
funding.dateRange: No comparable public value available for this filter.
funding.confidenceLabel: No comparable public value available for this filter.

insights.question: what-changed
insights.headline: Public media attention is broadly stable.
insights.shortAnswer: Public reporting on Sudan decreased by 2.5% compared with the previous 30 days.
evidenceBullets: 1,916 article-count mentions in the last 30 days.
1,966 article-count mentions in the previous 30 days.
5,944 article-count mentions across the last 90 days.
insights.caveat: Article volume is an attention measure. It does not verify a change in need or severity.

insights.question: under-reported
insights.headline: The clearest comparable movement is public attention, not funding coverage.
insights.shortAnswer: Sudan accounts for No comparable public value available of selected-country media mentions in the latest 30-day window, compared with Haiti.
evidenceBullets: Funding coverage is not comparable from the connected public data.
insights.caveat: A larger media share can reflect attention, language, or publication patterns. It should not be read as a ranking of humanitarian need.

insights.question: available-to-compare
insights.headline: Market data coverage is visible, but price movement is not inferred.
insights.shortAnswer: Sudan has public food-market coverage across 7 market references and 7 commodity references in the connected data search.
insights.caveat: Do not treat dataset coverage as evidence of price movement until commodity-level values are parsed.

insights.question: largest-gap
insights.headline: The food-price gap is commodity-level movement.
insights.shortAnswer: Sudan has market-data coverage, but the generated public values do not yet support a safe month-to-month or year-to-year price-change conclusion.
evidenceBullets: Price movement is not shown unless commodity-level values can be parsed without guessing.
insights.comparisonBasis: Coverage compared with extractable price movement
insights.caveat: Market coverage supports further comparison; it is not evidence that prices rose or fell.

insights.question: available-to-compare
insights.headline: The strongest comparable base is coverage of public datasets.
insights.shortAnswer: Sudan has 7 structured humanitarian dataset matches and 7 food-market references in the current build.
evidenceBullets: Media movement is also available for comparison.
insights.caveat: Coverage indicates what can be compared. It is not a severity score.

insights.question: largest-gap
insights.headline: The largest analytical gap is funding and reporting comparability.
insights.shortAnswer: Sudan has comparable public data coverage for market and humanitarian dataset availability, but funding coverage and humanitarian report movement are not comparable in the generated data.
evidenceBullets: Funding coverage is not shown because requirement and received values could not be compared.
Report movement is not shown because comparable report counts were not returned.
insights.comparisonBasis: Available values compared with missing comparable values
insights.caveat: A missing comparable value is not evidence of absence. It only marks the limit of this public-data view.

sourceGroups.label: Funding data
sourceGroups.dateRange: No comparable public value available for this filter.
sourceGroups.contribution: Funding coverage was not shown because comparable requirement and received values were not available.
sourceGroups.limitation: Funding data is shown only when requirement and received values can be compared without guessing.

sourceGroups.label: Media/article counts
sourceGroups.contribution: Used to calculate article-count movement and country share.
sourceGroups.limitation: Article counts are contextual attention signals, not confirmation of humanitarian conditions.

sourceGroups.label: Humanitarian reporting
sourceGroups.contribution: Reporting pressure was not shown because the API did not provide a comparable value.
sourceGroups.limitation: Report volume reflects publication behaviour and access to public reporting, not severity by itself.

sourceGroups.label: Food/market data
sourceGroups.limitation: Dataset coverage is not food-price severity unless commodity-level prices are parsed.

sourceGroups.label: Structured humanitarian indicators
sourceGroups.contribution: Used to show comparable dataset coverage.
sourceGroups.limitation: Dataset availability indicates comparability; it is not an indicator value.

countries.dataQuality: Multiple public categories produced comparable values.
